title: Writing - Part 1: Crime Prevention
description: Your class has had a discussion about crime prevention and the most effective ways to make communities safer.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the approaches in your notes. You should explain which approach you think would be most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: A visible police presence reassures local residents and deters potential criminals.
Addressing poverty, unemployment and education can tackle the root causes of crime.
Severe punishments send a clear message that criminal behaviour will not be tolerated.

structure.intro: Introduce the challenge of crime prevention and state the two approaches you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first approach and the evidence for its effectiveness.
structure.body2: Discuss the second approach and compare its merits to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which approach you believe is most effective and why.

content.modelAnswer:
Reducing crime is a priority for governments and communities worldwide. While there are many possible strategies, two of the most debated are increasing police presence and investing in community programmes that address the root causes of criminal behaviour.

A stronger police presence can act as a visible deterrent. When potential criminals know that officers are regularly patrolling an area, they may be less willing to take risks. Faster response times also help to reassure residents and build trust between the police and the community.

However, community programmes tackle the underlying conditions that lead people to commit crimes in the first place. Poverty, lack of education and unemployment are strongly correlated with criminal behaviour. Youth clubs, job training schemes and addiction support services provide alternatives and opportunities, making crime less appealing or necessary.

In my opinion, community programmes are the more effective long-term approach. While policing can manage immediate risks, it does not change the circumstances that lead people to offend. Addressing inequality and providing support for vulnerable people attacks the problem at its root, offering a more sustainable reduction in crime over time.
